diagrams.title: Class as Blueprint - Object Instances
diagrams.description: A visual representation showing a single class serving as a blueprint from which multiple object instances are created. This helps learners understand the relationship between a class definition and the objects instantiated from it.
elements: Central rectangle labeled 'Class: Dog' with attribute list (name, breed, age) and method list (bark, fetch)
Three smaller rectangles to the right representing objects: 'Object 1: Buddy', 'Object 2: Max', 'Object 3: Bella'
Arrow from class pointing to each object labeled 'instantiation'
Dotted line connecting class to objects showing they inherit the same structure
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates that a class is a template/blueprint and objects are concrete instances created from that blueprint with their own data values

diagrams.title: Class Structure Anatomy
diagrams.description: A diagram showing the internal structure of a Python class, separating class variables, instance variables, methods, and the special __init__ method. This helps students understand what components make up a class.
elements: Top section labeled 'Class Variables' with example 'species = 'mammal''
Middle section labeled '__init__ (Constructor)' with parameters 'self, name, age'
Lower section labeled 'Methods' with example methods 'eat()', 'sleep()', 'play()'
Instance variables shown in a callout as 'self.name, self.age'
Color-coded sections distinguishing data (attributes) from behavior (methods)
diagrams.purpose: Explains the anatomy of a Python class including attributes (both class and instance variables) and methods, helping learners recognize the building blocks of OOP

diagrams.title: Object Creation Process (Instantiation)
diagrams.description: A step-by-step flow diagram showing how an object is created from a class in Python, including the __init__ method call and the self parameter binding. This clarifies the instantiation process.
elements: Left box labeled 'Class Definition' showing the Dog class code
Arrow labeled '1. dog = Dog()' pointing to middle step
Arrow labeled '2. Create empty object' to step showing new object allocation
Arrow labeled '3. Bind self to object' showing self reference
Right box labeled 'Object Returned' showing the completed dog object with name='Rex', age=3
Arrow showing 'dog variable points to object'
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes the object instantiation process in Python, showing how __init__ works and how attributes get assigned to create a complete object
